preamble: The close-mid front unrounded vowel /e/ can undergo diphthongization to the diphthong /uo/, particularly in specific phonological contexts such as pretonic syllables.
phoneticEffects: This transformation involves diphthongization, a shift in vowel height (from close-mid to a diphthong starting with a high-back vowel), a change in backness (from front to back), and the introduction of rounding
sources: Bethin, C. Y. (1998). Slavic Prosody: Language Change and Phonological Theory. Cambridge University Press.
